manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 10073

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A08": 132, "B95": 800, "A39": 708, "A65": 302, "B81": 983, "B34": 420, "A28": 623, "C82": 349, "B69": 239, "A87": 28, "B64": 986, "B15": 660, "C79": 718, "B16": 707, "C53": 188, "C83": 774, "C10": 785, "A97": 775, "A25": 517, "C84": 768, "A71": 588, "B23": 657, "B96": 375, "C51": 310, "C17": 301, "B19": 388, "C72": 431, "A94": 950, "A46": 540, "B43": 997, "B37": 473, "A88": 831, "A96": 888, "C60": 879, "A75": 76}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A08": 231, "B95": 899, "A39": 807, "A65": 401, "B81": 1082, "B34": 519, "A28": 722, "C82": 448, "B69": 338, "A87": 127, "B64": 1085, "B15": 759, "C79": 817, "B16": 806, "C53": 287, "C83": 873, "C10": 884, "A97": 874, "A25": 616, "C84": 867, "A71": 687, "B23": 756, "B96": 474, "C51": 409, "C17": 400, "B19": 487, "C72": 530, "A94": 1049, "A46": 639, "B43": 1096, "B37": 572, "A88": 930, "A96": 987, "C60": 978, "A75": 175}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

We need to understand the problem: We are playing a collaborative negotiation game. The goal: maximize total importance to us (the participant) while staying within effort limit 10073. We have a set of items with effort values (given) and importance values (our personal view). We need to negotiate with another participant (the opponent) to agree on a set of items to keep. Both participants have their own importance values (unknown to each other). They also have effort values (same for both). The total effort of selected items must be ≤ 10073.

We need to propose sets, argue, etc. The game ends when one side agrees to the other's proposal.

We need to produce a message that includes STRATEGIC REASONING (once at beginning), ARGUMENT (somewhere), and possibly PROPOSAL, REFUSE, AGREE, etc. The content we give to the other participant must be non-empty. The strategic reasoning is hidden from the other participant.

We need to decide on a strategy. Since we don't know the opponent's importance values, we need to try to maximize our own importance while staying within effort limit. However, we also need to consider that the opponent may have different preferences. The negotiation may involve trade-offs.

We need to produce a message that includes at least an ARGUMENT and optionally a PROPOSAL. The problem statement says "You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages." So we must include ARGUMENT: {...} with some argument. The argument can be any string.

We also need to include STRATEGIC REASONING exactly once at the beginning of the message. It will not be shown to the other participant.

We need to decide what to propose. We could propose a set of items that gives us high importance and stays within limit. We could also try to anticipate opponent's preferences and propose a set that might be acceptable.

We need to decide on a set of items that maximizes our importance given the effort constraint. This is a knapsack problem: maximize sum of importance values subject to sum of effort ≤ 10073.

We need to solve the knapsack problem. Since we have 36 items, we can attempt to solve it manually using some heuristics or try to find the optimal solution using some systematic approach.

We can try to use a dynamic programming approach manually, but that would be time-consuming. However, we can try to approximate using greedy by ratio and then adjust.

Alternatively, we can try to solve the knapsack problem using integer linear programming (ILP) conceptually: maximize sum of importance_i * x_i subject to sum of effort_i * x_i ≤ 10073, x_i ∈ {0,1}.

We can try to solve this using branch and bound manually, but that's complex.

Given the context of the game, we don't need to find the absolute optimal solution; we need to propose a set that is good for us and plausible for the opponent.

We can also try to anticipate the opponent's preferences. Since we don't know their importance values, we might assume they have similar preferences (maybe they also value high-importance items). Alternatively, we could propose a set that includes items with high ratio of importance to effort, which might be more efficient for both parties.

We could also try to propose a set that is close to the Pareto optimal for both parties, but we don't know opponent's values.

Given the game, we can try to be strategic: we can propose a set that is beneficial to us and also not too bad for the opponent (i.e., includes items that are likely to be valuable to them). But we can try to propose a set that includes items with high importance to us and also relatively low effort (so we can include many items). This might be acceptable to the opponent because they might also value high-importance items.

Alternatively, we could propose a set that includes all items with effort less than some threshold, or we could propose a set that includes all items except some low-importance high-effort items.

reasoning_details.text:
We need to understand the problem: We are playing a collaborative negotiation game. The goal: maximize total importance to us (the participant) while staying within effort limit 10073. We have a set of items with effort values (given) and importance values (our personal view). We need to negotiate with another participant (the opponent) to agree on a set of items to keep. Both participants have their own importance values (unknown to each other). They also have effort values (same for both). The total effort of selected items must be ≤ 10073.

We need to propose sets, argue, etc. The game ends when one side agrees to the other's proposal.

We need to produce a message that includes STRATEGIC REASONING (once at beginning), ARGUMENT (somewhere), and possibly PROPOSAL, REFUSE, AGREE, etc. The content we give to the other participant must be non-empty. The strategic reasoning is hidden from the other participant.

We need to decide on a strategy. Since we don't know the opponent's importance values, we need to try to maximize our own importance while staying within effort limit. However, we also need to consider that the opponent may have different preferences. The negotiation may involve trade-offs.

We need to produce a message that includes at least an ARGUMENT and optionally a PROPOSAL. The problem statement says "You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages." So we must include ARGUMENT: {...} with some argument. The argument can be any string.

We also need to include STRATEGIC REASONING exactly once at the beginning of the message. It will not be shown to the other participant.

We need to decide what to propose. We could propose a set of items that gives us high importance and stays within limit. We could also try to anticipate opponent's preferences and propose a set that might be acceptable.

We need to decide on a set of items that maximizes our importance given the effort constraint. This is a knapsack problem: maximize sum of importance values subject to sum of effort ≤ 10073.

We need to solve this knapsack problem for our own importance values.

You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 10073

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A08": 132, "B95": 800, "A39": 708, "A65": 302, "B81": 983, "B34": 420, "A28": 623, "C82": 349, "B69": 239, "A87": 28, "B64": 986, "B15": 660, "C79": 718, "B16": 707, "C53": 188, "C83": 774, "C10": 785, "A97": 775, "A25": 517, "C84": 768, "A71": 588, "B23": 657, "B96": 375, "C51": 310, "C17": 301, "B19": 388, "C72": 431, "A94": 950, "A46": 540, "B43": 997, "B37": 473, "A88": 831, "A96": 888, "C60": 879, "A75": 76}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A08": 231, "B95": 899, "A39": 807, "A65": 401, "B81": 1082, "B34": 519, "A28": 722, "C82": 448, "B69": 338, "A87": 127, "B64": 1085, "B15": 759, "C79": 817, "B16": 806, "C53": 287, "C83": 873, "C10": 884, "A97": 874, "A25": 616, "C84": 867, "A71": 687, "B23": 756, "B96": 474, "C51": 409, "C17": 400, "B19": 487, "C72": 530, "A94": 1049, "A46": 639, "B43": 1096, "B37": 572, "A88": 930, "A96": 987, "C60": 978, "A75": 175}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

You will now receive the first message of the other player.

PROPOSAL: {'A87', 'A75', 'C53', 'B69', 'A65', 'C17', 'C51', 'C82', 'B96', 'B19', 'B34', 'C72', 'B37', 'A25', 'A46', 'A71', 'A28', 'B23', 'B15', 'B95', 'A39', 'B16', 'A08'}

ARGUMENT: {'This selection respects the shared effort limit (total effort 10073) and provides a high total importance of 11858 for me. It includes many items with high importance-to-effort ratios, making it efficient. I am open to discussing swaps if you have high-value items that conflict with your preferences.'}

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.

LIMIT = 10073

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
